inference_unit.notations: - $Hg-1201$: $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ - $Tl-1201$: $TlBa_{2}CuO_{5}$ - $Hg-1212$: $HgBa_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ - $Hg-1223$: $HgBa_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{8+x}$ - $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature - $\rho$: average mass density - $n$: number of $CuO_{2}$ layers per unit cell

premises.conjecture: The structural similarity between the mercury-based cuprate $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ (Hg-1201) and the thallium-based cuprate $TlBa_{2}CuO_{5}$ (Tl-1201) suggests that a homologous series of compounds exists in the Hg-Ba-Ca-Cu-O system with the general chemical composition $HgBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+2+\delta}$, where $n$ is an integer representing the number of $CuO_{2}$ layers per unit cell. [1]

conjecture.title: Correlation between Tc and CuO layers

premises.conjecture: In the Hg-Ba-Ca-Cu-O system, the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ is expected to increase as the number of $Cu$-$O$ planes per unit cell increases, consistent with trends observed in analogous thallium-based and bismuth-based copper oxide superconducting systems. [5]

The specific superconducting phase $HgBa_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{8+x}$ (Hg-1223), which contains three $CuO_{2}$ layers per unit cell, is the phase responsible for the superconductivity observed at approximately $133$ K in Hg-Ba-Ca-Cu-O samples.
premises.assumption: For the purpose of estimating the superconducting volume fraction from magnetic screening (zero-field cooling susceptibility) data in Hg-Ba-Ca-Cu-O composite samples, the average mass density of the material is assumed to be $\rho \approx 6~g~cm^{-3}$.
